title: Promoting Access to Mortgage Credit
summary: Executive Order 14393, issued on March 13, 2026, aims to promote access to mortgage credit by reducing regulatory burdens that have impacted community banks and creditworthy borrowers. The order directs various financial regulatory bodies, including the CFPB, Federal Reserve, NCUA, FDIC, OCC, and FHFA, to consider reforms in several key areas. These include tailoring Ability-to-Repay (ATR) and Qualified Mortgage (QM) requirements for smaller banks, modernizing Home Mortgage Disclosure Act (HMDA) data collection, aligning capital and liquidity frameworks, simplifying appraisal processes, and promoting digital mortgage modernization. Key findings and implications include an emphasis on shifting supervisory focus from technical compliance to the effectiveness of underwriting and borrower repayment ability, with enforcement reserved for actual borrower harm. The order also seeks to streamline processes, reduce compliance costs, and foster competition in the mortgage market to improve affordability and availability of credit, particularly for rural and low- to moderate-income households.
